_comment: Mutation testing — scoped to the pure logic: the shared domain core (shared/src/lib + shared/src/domain, 'the product') plus the pure .ts helpers behind the schedule (src/components/scheduler, src/lib) and the reset-page failure mapper (src/auth/resetPasswordFailure.ts — pure and unit-pinned, so its tests should bite too). React hooks are explicitly excluded: their effect/event orchestration is exercised by component and cross-browser tests, while mutating it produces lifecycle-noise and runner errors rather than isolated pure-function evidence. Answers 'do the unit tests actually BITE?' (kill mutants) rather than just 'did they run the lines?' (coverage). Deliberately NOT wired into the gate (runs take many minutes); run on demand with `pnpm run mutation` before releases or after core changes. Report lands in reports/mutation/mutation.html. `plugins` is EXPLICIT because Stryker's auto-discovery scans a flat node_modules/@stryker-mutator/* — under pnpm's nested layout it finds nothing and dies with 'no TestRunner plugins were loaded'.
